Name: Caterpie
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When attacked by bird Pokémon, it resists by releasing a terrifically strong odor from its antennae, but it often becomes their prey.

Name: Metapod
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its shell is filled with its soft innards. It doesn’t move much because of the risk it might carelessly spill its innards out.

Name: Butterfree
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Close examination of its large eyes reveals that each eye is composed of a myriad of tiny eyes.

Name: Paras
Flavor-Text: No matter how much it eats, the mushrooms growing on its back steal away most of the nutrients it consumes.

Name: Parasect
Effects: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 60 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 120 damage.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The large mushroom on its back controls it. It often fights over territory with Shiinotic.

Name: Pinsir
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, put your opponent’s Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: One solid blow from its horns is enough to split apart a large tree. Its greatest rival in Alola is Vikavolt.

Name: Surskit
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When this Pokémon senses danger, a sweet fluid oozes from the tip of its heads. The taste of it disgusts bird Pokémon.

Name: Masquerain
Effects: Move an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its wings and antennae don’t cope well with moisture. After a rain, it faces sunward to dry off.

Name: Rowlet
Flavor-Text: This wary Pokémon uses photosynthesis to store up energy during the day, while becoming active at night.

Name: Dartrix
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A bit of a dandy, it spends its free time preening its wings. Its preoccupation with any dirt on its plumage can leave it unable to battle.

Name: Decidueye
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It fires arrow quills from its wings with such precision, they can pierce a pebble at distances over a hundred yards.

Name: Decidueye-GX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: Put 3 cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Grubbin
Flavor-Text: They often gather near places frequented by electric Pokémon in order to avoid being attacked by bird Pokémon.

Name: Fomantis
Effects: Search your deck for a [G] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: They give off a sweet and refreshing scent. Cutiefly often gather near the tall grass where Fomantis are hiding.

Name: Lurantis-GX
Effects: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
This attack does 50 damage times the amount of [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Morelull
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: As it drowses the day away, it nourishes itself by sucking from tree roots. It wakens at the fall of night, wandering off in search of a new tree.

Name: Shiinotic
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a [G] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It emits flickering spores that cause drowsiness. When its prey succumb to sleep, this Pokémon feeds on them by sucking in their energy.

Name: Bounsweet
Effects: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A delectable aroma pours from its body. They are often swallowed whole by Toucannon lured by that wafting deliciousness.

Name: Steenee
Effects: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The sepals on its head developed to protect its body. These are quite hard, so even if pecked by bird Pokémon, this Pokémon is totally fine.

Name: Tsareena
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may have your opponent reveal their hand. Then, discard a card from it.
Effects: Heal 20 damage and remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its long, striking legs aren’t just for show but to be used to kick with skill. In victory, it shows off by kicking the defeated, laughing boisterously.

Name: Growlithe
Effects: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It’s both clever and loyal, but if a stranger tries to invade its territory, it barks threateningly.

Name: Arcanine
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Discard 3 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Overflowing with beauty and majesty, this strong Pokémon appears in ancient Eastern folklore.

Name: Torkoal
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Coal is the source of Torkoal’s energy. Large amounts of coal can be found in the mounts where they live.

Name: Litten
Flavor-Text: While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs.

Name: Torracat
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: At its throat, it bears a bell of fire. The bell rings brightly whenever this Pokémon spits fire.

Name: Incineroar
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has a violent, selfish disposition. If it’s not in the mood to listen, it will ignore its Trainer’s orders with complete nonchalance.

Name: Incineroar-GX
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched [R] Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Psyduck
Effects: Both Active Pokémon are now Confused.
Flavor-Text: As a result of headaches so fierce they cause it to cry, it sometimes uses psychokinesis without meaning to.

Name: Golduck
Effects: Discard up to 2 [W] Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: It is said that the red part of its forehead grants supernatural powers to those who posses one, so it was over-hunted in the past.

Name: Poliwag
Effects: This attack does more damage times the amount of [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s still not very good at walking. Its Trainers should train this Pokémon to walk every day.

Name: Poliwhirl
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It marches over the land in search of bug Pokémon to eat. Then it takes them underwater so it can dine on them where it’s safe.

Name: Poliwrath
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 80 more damage. Then, remove all Special Conditions from that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its percentage of body fat is nearly zero. Its body is entirely muscle, which makes it heavy and forces its swimming prowess to develop.

Name: Shellder
Flavor-Text: The hardness of its shell surpasses the hardness of a diamond. In days gone by, people used the shells to make shields.

Name: Cloyster
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage. If tails, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its hard shell cannot be shattered—not even by a bomb. The contents of the shell remain unknown.

Name: Lapras-GX
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Corsola
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Pursued by Mareanie for the branches on its head, this Pokémon will sometimes snap its own branches off as a diversion while it escapes.

Name: Wingull
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon. It can’t retreat during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: Fishermen keep an eye out for Wingull in the sky, because wherever they’re circling, the ocean sure to be is teeming with fish Pokémon.

Name: Pelipper
Effects: Put 1 of your Benched Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Gathering food is the work of young males. They store food in their capacious beaks and carry it back to others waiting in the nest.

Name: Popplio
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon snorts body fluids from its nose, blowing balloons to smash into its foes. It’s famous for being a hard worker.

Name: Brionne
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: A skillful dancer, it creates a sequence of water balloons as it dances, and briskly bombards it enemies.

Name: Primarina
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It controls its water balloons with song. The melody is learning from others of its kind and is passed down from one generation to the next.

Name: Primarina-GX
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of [W] Energy attached to your Pokémon.
Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Heal all damage from all of your Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Crabominable
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It just throws punches indiscriminately. In times of desperation, it can lop off its own pincers and fire them like rockets.

Name: Wishiwashi
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard all cards attached to this Pokémon and return it to your hand. You can’t use this Ability during your first turn or on the turn this Pokémon was put into play.
Flavor-Text: When it’s in trouble, its eyes moisten and begin to shine. The shining light attracts its comrades, and they stand together against their enemies.

Name: Dewpider
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It crawls onto the land in search of food. Its water bubble allows it to breathe and protects its soft head.

Name: Araquanid
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s [R] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It delivers headbutts with the water bubble on its head. Small Pokémon get sucked into the bubble, where they drown.

Name: Pyukumuku
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, put 6 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It lives in shallow seas, such as areas near a beach. It can eject its internal organs, which it uses to engulf its prey or battle enemies.

Name: Bruxish
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It stuns its prey with psychokinesis and then grinds them to mush with its strong teeth. Even Shellder’s shell is no match for it.

Name: Chinchou
Effects: Look at 1 of your face-down Prize cards.
Flavor-Text: It lives in the depths beyond the reach of sunlight. It flashes lights on its antennae to communicate with others of its kind.

Name: Lanturn
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
If this Pokémon has any [W] Energy attached to it, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon flashes a bright light that blinds its prey. This creates an opening for it to deliver an electrical attack.

Name: Charjabug
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: From the food it digests, it generates electricity, and it stores this energy in its electric sac.

Name: Vikavolt
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a [G] Energy card and a [L] Energy card and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It produces electricity via an electrical organ in its abdomen. It overwhelms bird Pokémon with shocking beams of electrical energy.

Name: Togedemaru
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.
Discard all [L] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: The long hairs on its back act as lightning rods. The bolts of lightning it attracts are stored as energy in its electric sac.

Name: Zubat
Effects: Choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.
Flavor-Text: It sleeps in caves during the day. It has no eyes, so to check its surroundings while flying, it emits ultrasonic waves.

Name: Golbat
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its thick fangs are hollow like straws, making them unexpectedly fragile. These fangs are specialized for sucking blood.

Name: Crobat
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 3 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Both its legs became wings, and as a result, it can’t move well on the ground. All it can do is crawl around.

Name: Alolan Grimer
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The crystals on Grimer’s body are lumps of toxins. If one falls off, lethal poisons leak out.

Name: Alolan Muk
Ability-Effect: Each Basic Pokémon in play, in each player’s hand, and in each player’s discard pile has no Abilities.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: While it’s unexpectedly quiet and friendly, if it’s not fed any trash for a while, it will smash its Trainer’s furnishings and eat up the fragments.

Name: Drowzee
Effects: This attack does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It finds really fun dreams tasty. When it makes friends with people, it may show them the most delicious dreams it’s ever eaten.

Name: Hypno
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: As a matter of course, it makes anyone it meets fall asleep and has a taste of their dreams. Anyone having a good dream, it carries off.

Name: Espeon-GX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
This attack does 30 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Put 10 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mareanie
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It plunges the poison spike on its head into its prey. When the prey has weakened, Mareanie deals the finishing blow with its 10 tentacles.

Name: Toxapex
Ability-Effect: Whenever your opponent’s Active Pokémon retreats, their new Active Pokémon is Poisoned.
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Toxapex crawls along the ocean floor on its 12 legs. It leaves a trail of Corsola bits scattered in its wake.

Name: Cosmog
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Its body is gaseous and frail. It slowly grows as it collects dust from the atmosphere.

Name: Cosmoem
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Motionless as if dead, its body is faintly warm to the touch. In the distant past, it was called the cocoon of the stars.

Name: Lunala-GX
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [P] Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t be healed during your opponent’s next turn.
Knock Out 1 of your opponent’s Basic Pokémon that isn’t a Pokémon-GX. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Makuhita
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It was originally brought in from another region, but now Makuhita from Alola are more famous.

Name: Hariyama
Effects: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is known for its fantastic strength, but as it grows older, it focuses more on training Makuhita.

Name: Roggenrola
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has [F] Resistance, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The hexagonal cavity is its ear. It walks in the direction of sounds it hears, but if the sounds cease, it panics and topples over.

Name: Boldore
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It explores caves in search of underground water. It’s not comfortable around water, so this Pokémon takes great care in lapping it up.

Name: Gigalith
Effects: Discard any amount of [F] Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Known for its hefty horsepower, this Pokémon is a popular partner for construction workers.

Name: Crabrawler
Flavor-Text: It punches so much, its pincers often come off from overuse, but they grow back quickly. What little meat they contain is rich and delicious.

Name: Passimian
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched Passimian.
Flavor-Text: They form groups of roughly 20 individuals. Their mutual bond is remarkable—they will never let down a comrade.

Name: Sandygast
Effects: Attach a [F] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It takes control of anyone who puts a hand in its mouth. And so it adds to the accumulation of its sand-mound body.

Name: Palossand
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Buried beneath the castle are masses of dried-up bones from those whose vitality it has drained.

Name: Alolan Rattata
Flavor-Text: When the sun goes down, it becomes active. It runs around town on a chase for good food for the boss of its nest—Raticate.

Name: Alolan Raticate
Effects: Search your deck for a number of cards up to the number of your Benched Pokémon and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This gourmet Pokémon is particular about the taste and freshness of its food. Restaurants where Raticate live have a good reputation.

Name: Alolan Meowth
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was not originally found in Alola. Human actions caused a surge in their numbers, and they went feral. They’re prideful and crafty.

Name: Alolan Persian
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its round face and smooth coat—softer than the most high-class velvet—have made this a very popular Pokémon in Alola.

Name: Umbreon-GX
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard 2 Energy from your opponent’s Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Carvanha
Effects: Discard all Pokémon Tool cards from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If they scent the faintest trace of blood, they rush to attack en masse. When alone, they’re rather cowardly.

Name: Sharpedo
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: It has a sad history. In the past, its dorsal fin was a treasured foodstuff, so this Pokémon became a victim of overfishing.

Name: Sandile
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.

Name: Krokorok
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: They move in groups of a few individuals. A female is often the leader of the group, and the males will gather food.

Name: Krookodile
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each card in your opponent’s hand.
Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its unique faculty of sight can detect small prey more than 30 miles away, even in the midst of a sandstorm.

Name: Alolan Diglett
Effects: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put them back in any order.
Flavor-Text: Its head sports an altered form of whiskers made of metal. When in communication with its comrades, its whiskers wobble to and fro.

Name: Alolan Dugtrio
Ability-Effect: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost is [C] more.
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Its shining gold hair provides it with protection. It’s reputed that keeping any of its fallen hairs will bring bad luck.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: Discard all Special Energy from each Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its feathers, which fall off as it grows, are thin and sharp. In times long past, warriors used them as swords.

Name: Solgaleo-GX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Search your deck for up to 5 Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Snubbull
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its growls make its opponents uneasy. This laid-back Pokémon tends to sleep half the day.

Name: Granbull
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: More timid than Snubbull, this Pokémon is doted on by young people amused at the contrast between its looks and its attitude.

Name: Cutiefly
Effects: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent’s next turn, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Flavor-Text: It feeds on the nectar and pollen of flowers. Because it’s able to sense auras, it can identify which flowers are about to bloom.

Name: Ribombee
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It rolls up pollen into puffs. It makes many different varieties, some used as food and others used in battle.

Name: Dratini
Effects: Search your deck for a Dratini, a Dragonair, and a Dragonite, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: After a 10-hour struggle, a fisherman was able to pull one up and confirm its existence.

Name: Dragonair
Effects: During your next turn, you may attach any number of Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: From time immemorial, it has been venerated by agricultural peoples as an entity able to control the weather.

Name: Dragonite
Effects: Discard a [G] Energy and a [L] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Incur the wrath of this normally calm Pokémon at your peril, because it will smash everything to smithereens before it’s satisfied.

Name: Spearow
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [G] Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Farmers whose fields are troubled by bug Pokémon appreciate Spearow for its vigorous appetite and look after it.

Name: Fearow
Effects: Your opponent shuffles their hand into their deck and draws 3 cards.
This Pokémon can’t use Slashing Strike during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: Drawings of a Pokémon resembling Fearow can be seen in murals from deep in ancient history.

Name: Kangaskhan
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Kangaskhan’s maternal love is so deep that it will brave death to protect its offspring.

Name: Tauros-GX
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Eevee
Ability-Effect: When you attach a basic Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon that is the same type as that Energy card and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Possessing an unbalanced and unstable genetic makeup, it conceals many possible evolutions.

Name: Spinda
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its steps are staggering and unsteady, but Spinda thinks it’s walking in a straight line.

Name: Lillipup
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Because it doesn’t yelp, it’s extremely popular with Trainers who live in apartment buildings.

Name: Herdier
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Its dense black fur grows continuously. The high cost of keeping its hard fur properly groomed make this a troublesome Pokémon to train.

Name: Stoutland
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks do 50 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Intelligent, good-natured, and valiant, it’s a trustworthy partner on rescue teams.

Name: Pikipek
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon feeds on berries, whose leftover seeds become the ammunition for the attacks it fires off from its mouth.

Name: Trumbeak
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: By bending its beak, it can produce a variety of call and brand itself a noisy nuisance for its neighbors.

Name: Toucannon
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Echoed Voice attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Within its beak, its internal gas ignites, explosively launching seeds with enough power to pulverize boulders.

Name: Yungoos
Flavor-Text: With its sharp fangs, it will bite anything. It did not originally live in Alola but was imported from another region.

Name: Gumshoos-GX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may have your opponent reveal their hand.
Effects: This attack does 50 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Stufful
Flavor-Text: Despite its adorable appearance, when it gets angry and flails about, its arms and legs could knock a pro wrestler sprawling.

Name: Bewear
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
You may do 40 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This immensely dangerous Pokémon possesses overwhelming physical strength. Its habitat is generally off-limits.

Name: Oranguru
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw cards until you have 3 cards in your hand.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Deep in the jungle, high in the lofty canopy, this Pokémon abides. On rare occasions, it shows up at the beach to match wits with Slowking.

Name: Big Malasada
Description: Heal 20 damage and remove a Special Condition from your Active Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Crushing Hammer
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Energy Retrieval
Description: Put 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Energy Switch
Description: Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Exp. Share
Description: When your Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, you may move 1 basic Energy card from that Pokémon to the Pokémon this card is attached to.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Great Ball
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Hau

Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Ilima

Name: Poison Barb
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Poké Ball
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Pokémon Catcher
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Potion
Description: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Professor Kukui
Description: Draw 2 cards. During this turn, your Pokémon’s attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Rare Candy
Description: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it. You can’t use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Repel
Description: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Rotom Dex
Description: After counting your Prize cards, shuffle them into your deck. Then, take that many cards from the top of your deck and put them face down as your Prize cards.
